evals.name: publish-markdown-as-draft
evals.prompt: Hey I wrote up release notes in this markdown file (release-notes.md). Can you stick it on my Ghost blog as a draft for now? The frontmatter has the tags already.
evals.expected_output: A draft post gets created on the blog via POST /posts/ with title 'Release notes for v2.3', status=draft, and the tags 'releases' and 'engineering' applied. The assistant reports back the resulting post URL/slug.
expectations: A POST request to /ghost/api/admin/posts/ was made exactly once
The POST request included ?source=html in the query string
The created post has title 'Release notes for v2.3'
The created post includes both 'releases' and 'engineering' tags
The assistant's final message tells the user the post was created (with slug or URL)

evals.name: list-recent-posts
evals.prompt: what are the 5 most recent posts on my ghost blog? just titles and status please
evals.expected_output: The assistant lists the seeded posts (Hello world, Why we switched to Ghost, Draft: Q2 roadmap) with their status, using list-posts or the /posts/ endpoint. No write operations.
expectations: A GET request to /ghost/api/admin/posts/ was made
No POST, PUT, or DELETE requests were made
The assistant's final message includes the title 'Hello, world'
The assistant's final message includes the title 'Why we switched to Ghost'
The assistant's final message mentions the status of at least one post

evals.name: add-tag-to-existing-post
evals.prompt: Add an 'announcements' tag to the post with slug 'hello-world' on my ghost blog. Keep the other tag it already has.
evals.expected_output: The assistant updates the post by fetching current state (to get updated_at and existing tags), then PUTs with both the existing tag ('meta') and the new one ('announcements').
expectations: At least one GET request to fetch the current post state was made
Exactly one PUT request to /ghost/api/admin/posts/... was made
The PUT request body included updated_at in the post object
The PUT request body's tags list includes both 'meta' and 'announcements'
The PUT request returned status 200 (not a 409 conflict or 422 validation error)
The assistant confirms the tag was added in its final message
